disclaimer.body: Informational only — not medical or financial advice. This page summarises publicly available information and links to official Singapore sources. Always confirm with your doctor and the official websites before making decisions.

benefits.title: Singapore Baby Benefits Centre
benefits.intro: What you're entitled to as a Singapore parent. Figures verified against official government sources.
benefits.sourcesNote: Figures verified against MOM, MSF, MOH/HealthHub and CPFB. Confirm co-matching and tax-relief figures against official pages before publishing.

explorePage.title: Interactive pregnancy guide

explorer.disclaimer: Informational only, not medical advice · prices are reference figures · confirm with your healthcare provider.

firstYears.title: Support through the first years
firstYears.intro: Government help doesn't stop at birth — here's what continues as your child grows.
firstYears.sourcesNote: Verified 2026 — confirm on IRAS / ECDA before relying on figures.

hospitalsPage.subtitle: Every Singapore maternity hospital. Delivery costs are 2026 reference figures from MOH Bill Benchmarks and published hospital rates (last reviewed June 2026) — guides, not quotes. Confirm with each hospital's bill estimator.
hospitalsPage.refNote: Reference figures (2026) — verify with the hospital.

calc.line_cdaMatch: CDA co-matching (max, if you save the cap)
calc.footnote: Indicative one-time support around birth; excludes ongoing subsidies, tax reliefs and annual credits.

cost.cashAvail: Baby Bonus Cash Gift available: {amount} (plus MediSave & CDA — see Benefits)
cost.footnote: You enter your own quotes; we never invent hospital prices. Hints are indicative ranges, not official figures. Saved to this device only.

productsPage.title: Baby essentials by stage
productsPage.subtitle: A curated starter list with indicative 2026 prices — so you buy what matters, when it matters.
productsPage.affiliate: Some links are affiliate links — we may earn a small commission at no extra cost to you. It never changes what we recommend. See our
productsPage.footnote: Prices are indicative (SGD, 2026) and vary by seller. Links open a Shopee search; add your affiliate tag in site.config.ts to earn commission.

stagePage.timingsNote: Timings are typical, not prescriptive — your gynae will guide what applies to you.

babyDev.factBorn: Congratulations — the beginning of a whole new adventure.
babyDev.factThird: Getting cozy and gaining weight, almost ready to meet you.
babyDev.factSecond: You might feel the first kicks — and baby can hear your voice.
babyDev.factFirst: Tiny but busy — the heart is beating and little features are forming.
babyDev.disclaimer: Illustrative development & fruit sizes are a friendly guide, not medical measurements.

calculatorPage.title: Pregnancy Cost Calculator
calculatorPage.subtitle: Build your own estimate. Enter the quotes you receive from your clinic and hospital — we total them and show the government support available to offset them.
calculatorPage.confinementDesc: The biggest post-birth cost — and MediSave doesn't cover it. Pick your options to estimate a realistic budget.
calculatorPage.deliveryPrompt: Looking for delivery-package prices? See the
calculatorPage.supportPrompt: page. Government support is detailed in

communityPage.title: Community & support
communityPage.subtitle: Peer support is one of the biggest helps in new parenthood. Here are trusted places to connect and get help.
communityPage.community0Desc: Long-running Singapore parenting forum — pregnancy, schools, everything.
communityPage.community2Desc: Several large, active local groups — search “Singapore Mums”.
communityPage.community3Desc: SG pregnancy & postpartum community with experts.
communityPage.community4Desc: Ask your hospital about new-parent groups and classes.
communityPage.communitiesNote: MySGBaby isn't affiliated with these communities — please follow each group's own rules. Seek groups that normalise the ups and downs, not milestone-boasting.
communityPage.breastfeedingNote: For breastfeeding support, ask your hospital's lactation service, or see
communityPage.faq0Q: Is it normal to feel overwhelmed?
communityPage.faq0A: Completely — the early weeks are a huge adjustment and most parents feel it. If low mood or anxiety persists, reach out to a helpline above or your doctor.
communityPage.faq1Q: How do I compare hospital costs?
communityPage.faq1A: See the Hospitals page for every SG maternity hospital with 2026 reference costs, and the Calculator to plan your budget.
communityPage.faq2Q: What support am I entitled to?
communityPage.faq2A: The Benefits Centre lists every grant, subsidy and tax relief — from birth to age 7 — with sources.
communityPage.faq3Q: I just found out I'm pregnant — where do I start?
communityPage.faq3A: Open the interactive guide and tap “Pre-conception & Testing”. It walks you through the very first steps.

onboardingPage.title: Let's personalise your guide
onboardingPage.subtitle: Six quick questions. Your answers stay on your device and help us show the right stage, costs and support for you.

toolsPage.title: Parent tools
toolsPage.subtitle: Simple trackers for the everyday moments — count your baby's kicks, and time contractions when the day comes. Saved to your device.

kick.intro: Tap each time you feel a movement. Aim for 10 in 2 hours.
kick.disclaimer: If you notice a clear reduction in your baby's movements, contact your provider straight away. This is a helper, not a medical device.

contraction.intro: Tap Start when a contraction begins, Stop when it ends.
contraction.guide: 5-1-1 guide: when contractions are ~5 min apart, last ~1 min, for 1 hour, it's usually time to head in. When in doubt, call your hospital.

babyLog.footnote: Saved to this device only. A handy log, not medical monitoring.

milestone.intro: Tick each milestone as your little one reaches it. Ranges are typical — every baby is different.
milestone.footnote: Ranges are a general guide (HealthHub). If you're ever concerned about your baby's development, speak to your doctor. Saved to this device.

confinement.cnyLabel: Due around Chinese New Year (nanny surcharge +$500–$1,000)
confinement.medisaveNote: MediSave does not cover confinement — budget for it separately. 2026 indicative ranges; prices rise over festive periods and vary by provider. Saved to this device only.

dueDate.lmpLabel: First day of your last period
dueDate.footnote: Estimate based on a 40-week (280-day) pregnancy from your last period. Your doctor's dating scan gives a more accurate date.

onboardingQuiz.tailorNote: We'll tailor what you see to your journey.
onboardingQuiz.footnote: Saved to this device only. No account needed.

consent.body: We use cookies and local storage to remember your preferences and, with your consent, to measure usage. See our <privacy>Privacy Policy</privacy>.

ivf.title: The IVF Journey
ivf.subtitle: If nature is taking a little longer, IVF is a hopeful, well-trodden path — and we'll walk every step of it with you.
ivf.intro: Here you'll find the whole IVF process laid out gently, step by step — what happens, how long it takes, and what it may cost in Singapore. Take your time, tap through at your own pace, and know we're here to give you as much information as you need.
ivf.reassure: You're not behind, and you're not alone — thousands of Singapore families grow this way every year. One step at a time.
ivf.stepDisclaimer: Informational only, not medical advice · every clinic tailors the plan to you · timings are typical, not fixed.
ivf.step0_what: Your first visit with a fertility specialist. They review your history and run a few tests — hormone bloods (like AMH), an ultrasound, and a semen analysis — to design a plan made just for you.
ivf.step0_note: This is your team getting to know you. No question is too small — bring your list and ask them everything.
ivf.step1_what: Daily hormone injections gently encourage your ovaries to grow several eggs at once. You'll pop in for short monitoring scans and blood tests every few days so your team can fine-tune the dose.
ivf.step1_note: The injections sound daunting, but most people find their rhythm within a few days. You've got this.
ivf.step2_what: A final 'trigger' injection ripens your eggs. About 36 hours later, the eggs are collected in a short day procedure under light sedation — you rest a while, then go home the same day.
ivf.step2_note: It's quicker than most expect. Plan a cosy rest day and someone kind to take you home.
ivf.step3_what: Your eggs meet sperm in the lab — either standard IVF, or ICSI, where a single sperm is placed into each egg. Embryologists nurture the embryos and watch them grow for three to five days.
ivf.step3_note: Behind the scenes, a whole team of specialists is quietly cheering your embryos on.
ivf.step4_what: A healthy embryo is gently placed into your womb — a quick, gentle procedure that usually needs no anaesthetic. Extra good-quality embryos can be frozen for future tries.
ivf.step4_note: A tiny seed of hope, settled right where it belongs.
ivf.step5_what: Now you wait, with medication to support the womb lining. After roughly two weeks, a blood test (beta-hCG) checks for pregnancy — far more reliable than an early home test.
ivf.step5_note: The hardest wait of all. Be gentle with yourself — rest, distract, and lean on your people.
ivf.step6_what: A positive result leads to an early viability scan a couple of weeks later. If this cycle doesn't work, your team reviews everything with you and plans the next step — often using the embryos you've frozen.
ivf.step6_note: Whatever the result, you're not back at square one — and you are never doing this alone.
ivf.costTitle: What might IVF cost in Singapore?
ivf.costIntro: Cost matters, and you deserve clear figures. Here's an honest, indicative picture — your clinic will give you an exact quote, and there's real government help to bring it down.
ivf.indicativeBadge: Indicative figures, 2026 — always confirm with MOH and your clinic
ivf.cost_public_note: One fresh cycle, before government co-funding
ivf.cost_fet_note: Per later transfer using a frozen embryo
ivf.supportHeading: Help that brings the cost down
ivf.support_cofund_note: Eligible Singaporean couples can receive co-funding for treatment at public ART centres (indicative up to ~S$7,700 per fresh cycle, less for frozen cycles). Confirm current limits and eligibility with MOH.
ivf.support_medisave_note: You can use MediSave to pay part of your treatment, within the per-cycle withdrawal limits. Confirm current caps with MOH/CPF.
ivf.verifyNote: These are indicative 2026 figures gathered from public sources to help you plan — not a quote. Fees, co-funding and MediSave limits change; always confirm the latest with MOH and your chosen clinic before making decisions.
ivf.closing: We're here to give you as much information as we can, whenever you need it. Take it one step at a time — hope is a plan too. 💛
ivf.closingSub: Every family's path is different. Whatever yours looks like, you're welcome here.

lossPage.eyebrow: A gentle space, just for you
lossPage.bannerBody: Whatever you're carrying today, you don't have to carry it by yourself. We're here — and so are people trained to help, any time you need them.
lossPage.intro: Every pregnancy journey is unique, and there is no blame in loss. Be gentle with yourself — there's no timeline for grief, and no wrong way to feel. You have not been forgotten here.
lossPage.affirm0: What you're feeling is valid — there's no right or wrong way to grieve.
lossPage.affirm1: This was not your fault. Loss is not a failure.
lossPage.affirm2: Take all the time you need. Grief doesn't follow a schedule.
lossPage.affirm3: You are still a parent, and your love still counts.
lossPage.affirm4: Leaning on others is strength, not weakness.
lossPage.affirm5: Whenever you're ready, hope is still here — and so are we.
lossPage.resourcesHeading: Singapore support, whenever you need it
lossPage.resourcesIntro: Reaching out is a brave first step. These lines are here for you — tap to call.
lossPage.res_sos_note: 24-hour emotional support if you're in distress.
lossPage.res_aware_note: A safe, confidential space to talk things through.
lossPage.res_hospital_note: Ask your care team about perinatal loss and counselling support — KKH, NUH and others offer it.
lossPage.crisisNote: If you are in crisis or need to talk to someone right now, please call a helpline or your doctor immediately. You deserve support today.
lossPage.verifyNote: Helpline details are indicative and should be re-confirmed before you rely on them. If a number has changed, your GP or hospital can point you to current services.
lossPage.readyBody: Many families go on to try again — in their own time, and often with a little help. If and when you're ready, we'll be right here with clear, gentle information, from natural conception to IVF. There is no pressure and no timeline. Hope is allowed to sit quietly beside grief.
lossPage.closing: You are not forgotten. You are not alone. And whenever you're ready, we'll walk the next step with you. 🤍
